title: Protocol for Assessing Phenoloxidase Activity in Galleria mellonella Hemolymph
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"hjxbj","text":"Bacterial or fungal infection in Galleria mellonella larvae produces an immune response in the host, affecting the antimicrobial enzymatic activity as the body tries to fight back the foreign elements causing the infection. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c2ubl","text":"This is a protocol to evaluate enzymatic activity of phenoloxidase in the cell-free hemolymph of Galleria mellonella larvae previously infected with a bacterial or fungal strain. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c5536","text":"I designed this experiment to be run in parallel and/or use the same hemolymph samples obtained with the \"Protocol for Assessing Lysozyme-Like Activity in Galleria mellonella Hemolymph\"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"701cn","text":"The anticoagulant buffer is taken from the described in DOI: 10.1159/000168009","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"blr2k","text":"Total Protein Quantification (for Normalization):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3buog","text":"Use a Bradford or BCA Protein Assay Kit (e.g., from Bio-Rad or Thermo Fisher).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3eji7","text":"Standards: Prepare a standard curve using Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"79ba5","text":"Follow the kit’s microplate protocol to determine the total protein concentration in the CFH samples. This will allow you to express lysozyme-like activity as \"Activity/mg protein,\" providing a more accurate assessment of enzyme activity, especially if sample collection varies.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"wgtue","text":"Prepare anticoagulant buffer 93 mM NaCl, 100 mM glucose, 30 mM trisodium citrate, 26 mM citric acid, 10 mM Na2EDTA, and 0.1 mM phenylthiourea, pH 4.6","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":109,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"xmnng","text":"Ensure all equipment and reagents are sterile to prevent contamination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bpswl","text":"It is crucial to keep all samples on ice until they are processed to maintain enzyme activity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3popo","text":"Anticoagulant Solution:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ucmk","text":"93 mM NaCl, 100 mM glucose, 30 mM trisodium citrate, 26 mM citric acid, 10 mM Na2EDTA, and 0.1 mM phenylthiourea, pH 4.6. Filter-sterilized through a 0.45 nm pore filter","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pp0ug","text":"L-Dopa Solution: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ifbi","text":"50 mM L-Dopa solution in PBS, stored it at -20°C until use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"drmsq","text":"Polyphenol Oxidase (Tyrosinase)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d63av","text":"Sterile PBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"au3t1","text":"1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9qeai","text":"Crushed ice tray","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2b86u","text":"Liquid nitrogen for snap-freezing","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"alg6a","text":"Refrigerated ultracentrifuge","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Surface sterilize the blades of a pair of micro scissors by dipping them in ethanol and allowing them to air dry.
Gently squeeze the larva to collect the dripping hemolymph (aim for 50-100 µL per larva) into a pre-chilled tube containing 50 µl of ice-cold anticoagulant buffer.
Centrifuge the tubes at 4°C, 4000 x g for 10 minutes to separate cellular debris from the hemolymph.
Aliquot the CFH to avoid freeze-thaw cycles.
Dilute the CFH sample in PBS buffer at a ratio of 1:10.
In one or several wells of a microplate, add 50 µl of the diluted CFH solution or PO standard.
Add 50 µl of the 50 µM L-Dopa solution to each well to start the enzymatic reaction. Use a multichannel pippette to start all reactions simultaneously.
Immediately measure the absorbance at 490 nm using a spectrophotometer or microplate reader.
Dispense 50 µl of anticoagulant buffer into 1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes, and register their respective mass (1 tube per larva).
Take the overnight bacterial culture and transfer 2 ml into an Eppendorf tube
Grow 3 ml of an overnight bacteria culture at 37 °C in an incubator with vigorous shaking (150-200 rpm)
Centrifuge at no more that 1000 rcf, for 10 min at 4 °C.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the bacterial pellet in 1 ml of sterile PBS
Measure the absorbance at 600 nm and dilute the suspension so it contains about 10^6 cells/ml
Select Galleria mellonela larvae in their 7th instar (usually, above 400 mg in weight) and distribute in Petri dishes as groups of 5 for each condition.
Prepare a Hamilton syringe with 10 µl of bacterial suspension or sterile PBS in case of control.
Grab a larva by its sides with the thumb and the index fingers and flip it upside down to expose its abdomen
Localize the hind-left proleg of the larva and carefully insert the needle of the syringe about 5 mm into the body of the larva
Inject the contents of the syringe slowly to prevent any leaking, and remove the syringe.
Return the larva to their respective dish and repeat for all the larvae and conditions.
Transfer the Petri dishes to an incubator and incubate the larvae for 0-6 hours at 37 °C
Optional: Anesthetize the larvae on ice for 5-10 minutes to minimize movement during hemolymph collection
Surface sterilize the larvae by dipping them in 70% ethanol for a few seconds, then rinse with sterile PBS or water.
Place the tube back on ice immediately after collection.
Repeat the collection steps for all the larvae.
With the scissors, make a small cut in a proleg of the anesthetized larva.
Register the weight and add ice cold anticoagulant buffer to ensure the final ratio of hemolymph to buffer is 1:1
Carefully transfer the clear supernatant (cell-free hemolymph) to a new pre-chilled tube.
Snap-freeze aliquots in liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C until ready for the PO activity assay.
Pre-incubate the mixture at room temperature for 5 minutes.
Record the absorbance every 30 seconds for a total duration of 30 minutes.
Calculate the slope of the linear increase in absorbance (ΔA 490 /min). This rate is proportional to the PO activity.
Normalize the PO activity to the total protein concentration in the CFH.
Transfer the tubes to an ice tray and wait a couple of minutes so the contents of the tubes are ice-cold.
Prepare a series of Polyphenol Oxidase dilutions in 0.1 M sodium phosphate buffer to create a standard curve (0, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 20 µg/ml).
creator: Juan Jose Quispe Haro
